chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P No.D-1462 of 2018 (Khadim Hussain & another v Federation of Pakistan & others) ______________________________________________________________ Date Order With Signature Of Judge ______________________________________________________________ Before:- Mr. Justice Adnan-ul-Karim Memon Mr. Justice Zulfiqar Ali Sangi Date of hearing and order:-28.04.2026 Mr. Imran Khan, advocate for the petitioners. Mr. Khalil Ahmed Dogar, advocate for respondents No.2 and 3. Mr. Muhammad Akbar Khan, Assistant Attorney General. ------------- O R D E R Adnan-ul-Karim Memon, J Learned counsel for the petitioners invited the Court’s attention to the comments filed on behalf of respondents No.2 and 3, wherein it has been categorically stated that the case for regularization of the petitioners had already been forwarded; however, time was sought to obtain instructions from the competent authority. Conversely, learned counsel for respondents No.2 and 3 submitted that there exists no concept of regularization within the Custom House Common Pool Trust and, therefore, there is no necessity to seek any instructions from the competent authority in this regard. At this juncture, learned counsel for the petitioners submitted that in the event the services of petitioner No.1 have been regularized, the case of petitioner No.2 may also be considered on the same footing. On this understanding, he stated that he does not wish to press the present petition. The request appears to be reasonable and is accordingly acceded to. Consequently, without touching the merits of the case, the instant petition is disposed of with the understanding that, if the services of petitioner No.1 have been regularized by the competent authority, the same treatment shall also be extended to petitioner No.2, subject to all just exceptions in accordance with law.

The request appears to be reasonable and is accordingly acceded to. Consequently, without touching the merits of the case, the instant petition is disposed of with the understanding that, if the services of petitioner No.1 have been regularized by the competent authority, the same treatment shall also be extended to petitioner No.2, subject to all just exceptions in accordance with law. JUDGE Shahzad Soomro JUDGE
